title: Fees for biocides - Canada.ca
content:
Information for the biocides industry about fees related to biocides, with links to supporting guidance and forms.
- Fee to evaluate biocides applications
- Fee for the right to sell biocides
- How fees are set and how to pay them

## Fee to evaluate biocides applications
Before a biocide is authorized for sale in Canada, Health Canada assesses its safety, efficacy and quality. Biocide products include disinfectants previously regulated under the Food and Drug Regulations , surface sanitizers for use in food premises previously subject to the Food and Drugs Act , and certain surface sanitizers previously regulated under the Pest Control Products Act . Further information can be found in:
- Biocide application review fees
- Guidance document: Evaluation fees for biocides
- Guidance document: Management of biocide applications

## Fee for the right to sell biocides
Health Canada monitors biocides on the Canadian market through post-market surveillance activities. Industry pays an annual fee for the right to sell biocides products and to maintain those products on the Canadian market. Further information can be found in:
- Fees for the right to sell biocides
- Guidance document: Biocide annual confirmation and fees for the right to sell biocides

## How fees are set and how to pay them
Fees for regulatory activities related to biocides are charged based on:
- Fees in Respect of Drugs and Medical Devices Order
For all other biocides guidance documents and forms for applications, please visit:
- Guidance on biocides
- Biocide Application Form
For information about paying invoices, please refer to How to pay fees .
